groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Subject, goal, audience, message, facts, format, CTA, language, length, perspective.

groups.title: Voice & Formatting
groups.description: Tone, brand voice, emoji use, and hashtags.

groups.title: Evidence & Restrictions
groups.description: Sources, link, disclosures, privacy, claim bans, extra rules.

variables.label: Subject, Product, or Update
variables.placeholder: What is the post about?
variables.hint: Enter the main topic, product, event, lesson, announcement, or professional update.

help.why: It establishes the central subject of the post.
help.example: How small teams publish honest product updates
help.avoid: Several unrelated topics in one post.

variables.label: Primary Goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result the post should support.

help.why: The primary goal determines the post’s structure, emphasis, and CTA.
help.example: Build authority
help.avoid: Stacking hire + sell + discuss as equal primary goals.

variables.label: Target Audience
variables.placeholder: Example: freelance designers, SaaS founders, HR managers
variables.hint: Describe the professionals or decision-makers the post should speak to.

help.why: Audience information materially changes vocabulary, examples, depth, and positioning.
help.example: Example: freelance designers, SaaS founders, HR managers
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Core Message
variables.placeholder: What should readers understand, remember, or believe after reading?
variables.hint: State the central insight, lesson, argument, announcement, or value proposition.

help.why: It prevents the model from guessing the intended takeaway.
help.example: One clear professional takeaway readers should remember
help.avoid: Guessing the takeaway or inventing lessons.

variables.label: Facts and Key Details
variables.placeholder: Add names, dates, features, results, examples, numbers, or confirmed details.
variables.hint: Include only facts that may safely appear in the post; write “No external claims” when appropriate.

help.why: It provides the factual basis and reduces unsupported claims or invented details.
help.example: No external claims — or only verified numbers and details
help.avoid: Invented metrics, clients, or personal stories.

variables.label: Post Format
variables.placeholder: Choose a format
variables.hint: Select a structure or let the model choose the best fit for the goal.

help.why: Different LinkedIn post types require meaningfully different narrative structures.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Reader Action
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred action
variables.hint: Select one primary next step or let the model decide whether a CTA is useful.

help.why: It controls the post’s conclusion without requiring the user to write a CTA.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Output Language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, German
variables.hint: Enter the language in which the complete post should be written.

help.why: Language cannot always be safely inferred from the topic or interface.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Post Length
variables.placeholder: Choose a preferred length
variables.hint: The model will prioritize completeness and remain below the platform limit.

help.why: Length materially affects depth, pacing, and mobile readability.
help.example: Platform appropriate
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Publishing Perspective
variables.placeholder: Choose who is publishing
variables.hint: Controls whether the post sounds personal, employee-led, or organizational.

help.why: Personal profiles and company Pages require different pronouns, credibility cues, and levels of formality.
help.example: Personal profile
help.avoid: Company Page voice on a personal confession without authorization.

variables.label: Audience Context
variables.placeholder: What does this audience already know about the subject?
variables.hint: Add relevant experience level, industry context, or current situation.

help.why: It helps prevent content from being too basic, technical, or disconnected from the reader’s reality.
help.example: What does this audience already know about the subject?
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Audience Problem or Interest
variables.placeholder: What problem, question, or opportunity matters most to them?
variables.hint: Explain why the audience should care about this post now.

help.why: It connects the subject to a concrete reader need rather than producing a self-focused post.
help.example: What problem, question, or opportunity matters most to them?
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Credibility Context
variables.placeholder: Add relevant experience, role, process, or verified result.
variables.hint: Provide only credibility details that may truthfully appear in the post.

help.why: It allows credible authority signals without encouraging invented expertise.
help.example: Add relevant experience, role, process, or verified result.
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Reader Questions or Objections
variables.placeholder: What might readers doubt, misunderstand, or disagree with?
variables.hint: The model may address relevant concerns naturally within the post.

help.why: It improves persuasion and clarity when the topic may generate predictable resistance.
help.example: What might readers doubt, misunderstand, or disagree with?
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose a tone
variables.hint: Select the general communication style without controlling every sentence.

help.why: Tone meaningfully changes how the same message is perceived.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Brand Voice Notes
variables.placeholder: Example: practical, slightly informal, no motivational language
variables.hint: Add distinctive voice preferences that cannot be inferred from the topic.

help.why: It supports recognizable brand style without creating several overlapping voice fields.
help.example: Example: practical, slightly informal, no motivational language
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Emoji Use
variables.placeholder: Choose emoji use
variables.hint: Controls whether emojis are omitted or used sparingly.

help.why: Emoji preferences vary significantly between professional brands and personal creators.
help.example: Minimal
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Hashtag Use
variables.placeholder: Choose hashtag behavior
variables.hint: Auto adds hashtags only when they provide clear relevance or discovery value.

help.why: Hashtags may be useful for some professional topics but inappropriate or distracting for others.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Source Material
variables.placeholder: Paste approved notes, research, product information, or source excerpts.
variables.hint: The model may summarize this material but must not invent missing information.

help.why: It provides additional evidence and detailed context for fact-sensitive posts.
help.example: Paste approved notes, research, product information, or source excerpts.
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Destination Link
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/page
variables.hint: Add the exact page readers should visit when the post requires a link.

help.why: The model must not invent URLs or guess the intended destination.
help.example: https://example.com/page
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Affiliation or Disclosure
variables.placeholder: Example: I built this product; sponsored by; affiliate relationship
variables.hint: Add any ownership, employment, sponsorship, or commercial relationship that should be disclosed.

help.why: It prevents promotional content from being presented as an independent recommendation.
help.example: I built this product / employed by …
help.avoid: Hidden commercial relationships.

variables.label: Privacy Restrictions
variables.placeholder: List names, client details, metrics, or internal information that must not appear.
variables.hint: The model will omit or generalize protected and confidential details.

help.why: Professional posts may involve clients, employees, internal data, or personal information.
help.example: Do not name the client; omit internal revenue
help.avoid: Leaving private client data in essentialFacts.

variables.label: Claims or Wording to Avoid
variables.placeholder: Example: no guarantees, no competitor comparisons, avoid “best”
variables.hint: List prohibited claims, sensitive terminology, or legal and brand restrictions.

help.why: It protects against known brand, regulatory, contractual, or factual risks.
help.example: Example: no guarantees, no competitor comparisons, avoid “best”
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.

variables.label: Additional Requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining context or formatting requirement.
variables.hint: Use this only for relevant instructions not covered by the other fields.

help.why: It provides one controlled place for legitimate requirements without creating miscellaneous duplicate fields.
help.example: Add any remaining context or formatting requirement.
help.avoid: Invented expertise, fake vulnerability, engagement bait, or private data.
